# Tabs

Switches between related content panels without leaving the current view.

## Contents

- [Installation](#installation)
- [Usage](#usage)
- [Selected state](#selected-state)
- [Variants](#variants)
- [URL-driven navigation](#url-driven-navigation)
- [Accessibility and composition](#accessibility-and-composition)

## Installation

Add the Tabs component from the configured StackTrace registry.

```shell
npx shadcn-vue@latest add @stacktrace/tabs
```

## Usage

Put `TabsTrigger` components inside `TabsList` and match every trigger value to exactly one `TabsContent` value. Use tabs for a small set of peer views that share one context. Prefer headings on one page when users need to compare sections, and use links when each destination needs its own URL and navigation history.

**Tabs example**

```vue
<template>
  <Tabs default-value="account" class="w-full max-w-md">
    <TabsList>
      <TabsTrigger value="account">Account</TabsTrigger>
      <TabsTrigger value="password">Password</TabsTrigger>
    </TabsList>
    <TabsContent value="account">
      <Card>
        <CardHeader>
          <CardTitle>Account</CardTitle>
          <CardDescription>Update your public account details.</CardDescription>
        </CardHeader>
        <CardContent><Input value="Ada Lovelace" /></CardContent>
      </Card>
    </TabsContent>
    <TabsContent value="password">
      <Card>
        <CardHeader>
          <CardTitle>Password</CardTitle>
          <CardDescription>Choose a secure password for this account.</CardDescription>
        </CardHeader>
        <CardContent><Input type="password" value="password" /></CardContent>
      </Card>
    </TabsContent>
  </Tabs>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import { Card, CardContent, CardDescription, CardHeader, CardTitle } from '@/Components/Card'
import { Input } from '@/Components/Input'
import { Tabs, TabsContent, TabsList, TabsTrigger } from '@/Components/Tabs'
</script>
```

## Selected state

Use `default-value` for initial uncontrolled state. Bind `v-model` when another control must select a panel or the application needs to observe the active value. Keep state that belongs to a panel outside Tabs if switching away must not discard it.

```vue
<Tabs v-model="section">
  <TabsList>...</TabsList>
  <TabsContent value="profile">...</TabsContent>
</Tabs>
```

Do not use a disabled tab to advertise content that does not exist. A disabled trigger is useful only when the panel will become available in the current workflow and the reason is clear nearby.

## Variants

The default variant places triggers on a muted track. Use `variant="ghost"` for lower-emphasis tabs in dense surfaces. Choose one variant per tab set and keep the active state visually distinct from hover and focus.

```vue
<Tabs default-value="overview" variant="ghost">
  <TabsList>...</TabsList>
  <TabsContent value="overview">...</TabsContent>
</Tabs>
```

## URL-driven navigation

StackTrace's `TabsLinkList` and `TabsLink` provide tab-like styling for Inertia navigation. They are links rather than a tab widget: each activation performs navigation, and active state is inferred from the current URL unless the `active` prop is set explicitly.

**Navigation links example**

```vue
<template>
  <TabsLinkList variant="ghost">
    <TabsLink href="/docs/components/tabs" :active="true">
      Overview
    </TabsLink>
    <TabsLink href="/docs/components/tabs?section=accessibility" :active="false">
      Accessibility
    </TabsLink>
    <TabsLink href="/docs/components/tabs?section=api" :active="false">
      API
    </TabsLink>
  </TabsLinkList>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import { TabsLink, TabsLinkList } from '@/Components/Tabs'
</script>
```

Use this link composition for sibling pages such as account sections. Use stateful `Tabs` when panels belong to one page and should switch without navigation.

## Accessibility and composition

Stateful Tabs supplies tablist, tab, and tabpanel semantics. Arrow keys move focus between triggers according to orientation, and the active trigger is associated with its panel. Keep trigger labels short and unique, preserve visible focus, and avoid interactive elements inside a trigger.

Link-based tab styling retains normal link keyboard behavior instead of tab-widget arrow navigation. When using `as-child` with a stateful trigger, the child must preserve roles, attributes, refs, and events described in the [composition fundamentals](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/fundamentals/composition).

Hosted documentation: [Tabs](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/tabs)
